query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Askance', 'Turned to the side, especially of the eyes', 'Critics argued that the plan might askance the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Askew', 'Turned or twisted to one side', 'Over time, the community learned to askew its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Asperity', 'Harshness of tone or manner', 'Her asperity became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aspersion', 'An attack on the reputation or integrity of someone', 'Her aspersion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aspiration', 'Strong desires or ambitions', '“Her aspirations to become a doctor motivated her to study diligently.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Aspire', 'To hope or dream, especially towards a profession', 'The author hoped the new policy would aspire tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assail', 'To attack violently with words or blows', 'Critics argued that the plan might assail the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assailant', 'Someone who attacks or assails another violently', 'Her assailant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assassin', 'To assassinate', 'Critics argued that the plan might assassin the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assay', 'To examine or analyze', 'Her assay became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assent', 'To agree to, give approval to', 'Critics argued that the plan might assent the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assert', 'To state or declare something forcefully', '“He asserted his right to speak during the meeting.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assess', 'To evaluate or analyze something', '“The teacher assessed the students’ essays carefully.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assessor', 'One who assesses a property for tax evaluation', 'Scholars noted a striking assessor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Asset', 'Something or someone of any value', 'Scholars noted a striking asset between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Asseverate', 'To declare earnestly, seriously, or positively, to affirm', 'The author hoped the new policy would asseverate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assiduous', 'Showing great care and perseverance', '“Her assiduous work ethic earned her a promotion at the company.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assignee', 'One to whom a thing is assigned', 'Critics argued that the plan might assignee the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assimilate', 'To take in and incorporate as one''s own', 'Over time, the community learned to assimilate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assonance', 'The repetition of similar or identical vowel sounds', 'Her assonance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assonant', 'Having successive similar vowel sounds', 'What seemed assonant at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assonate', 'To correspond in (particularly vowel) sounds', 'Over time, the community learned to assonate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assuage', 'To make an unpleasant feeling less intense; to relieve or soothe', 'Critics argued that the plan might assuage the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assumption', 'A belief accepted as true without proof', '“The assumption that it would not rain led them to leave their umbrellas at home.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Assurance', 'A declaration tending to inspire full confidence', 'Over time, the community learned to assurance its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Astound', 'To overwhelm with amazement', 'The author hoped the new policy would astound tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Astral', 'Relating to or resembling the stars, starry', 'The author hoped the new policy would astral tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Astringent', 'Sharp, caustic, severe', 'The reviewer described the tone as astringent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Astute', 'Having an ability to accurately assess situations', 'What seemed astute at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Asunder', 'Apart, divided', 'Scholars noted a striking asunder between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Asylum', 'A place of safety', 'The essay highlights the asylum of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atheist', 'Of or relating to atheists or atheism, atheistic', 'Over time, the community learned to atheist its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Athirst', 'Thirsty', 'Scholars noted a striking athirst between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Athwart', 'Across, crosswise', 'Scholars noted a striking athwart between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atomizer', 'An instrument for reducing a liquid to spray or vapor', 'Critics argued that the plan might atomizer the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atone', 'To make amends or reparation', 'Critics argued that the plan might atone the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atrocious', 'Frightful, evil, cruel or monstrous', 'The passage presents a atrocious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atrocity', 'An extremely wicked or cruel act', 'Scholars noted a striking atrocity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atrophy', 'To wither or waste away', 'Over time, the community learned to atrophy its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attache', 'An official associated with an embassy', 'Scholars noted a striking attache between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attain', 'To accomplish, to achieve', 'Critics argued that the plan might attain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attainment', 'The act of arriving at or reaching', 'The essay highlights the attainment of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attenuate', 'Weaken or reduce in force', 'Over time, the community learned to attenuate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attest', 'To serve as proof or evidence of something', '“The ancient ruins attest to the civilization’s advanced architectural skills.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attribute', 'To regard something as being caused by a particular source', '“He attributed his success to years of hard work and dedication.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Attrition', 'Wearing or grinding down by friction', 'Scholars noted a striking attrition between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Atypical', 'Not conforming to the normal type', 'Critics argued that the plan might atypical the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Auburn', 'Of a reddish-brown colour', 'Scholars noted a striking auburn between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Audacious', 'Showing a willingness to take bold risks', 'The passage presents a audacious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Audible', 'Capable of being heard', 'Her audible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
